content.text: Software delivery pipelines traditionally promote code from dev → staging → production with automated checks at each boundary. GenAI applications require additional gates at each promotion point because a change that passes functional tests can still degrade model behaviour, violate governance rules, or introduce security vulnerabilities that only emerge under production-like conditions.

This document defines the four-environment promotion pipeline for GenAI applications (Dev → Staging → Pre-prod/Canary → Production), the automated and manual gates at each boundary, the approval workflow, and the criteria for rolling back a promotion.

attrs.alt: Environment promotion pipeline: Dev → Staging → Pre-prod/Canary → Production with gates A, B, C

content.text: GenAI applications benefit from a four-stage promotion pipeline because each stage tests a different class of problem:
Fast feedback on code and prompt structure; CI gates 1–3
Eval suite against live model; load tests; smoke tests
ORR sign-off; 5% traffic canary; 24h soak period
Full traffic; monitored rollout; alerting active

Not all teams need all four environments. A low-risk internal tool may skip canary and go directly from staging to production with manual approval. The key principle: the more user-facing and consequential the LLM output, the more stages you need before full production traffic.

Gate A is fully automated. It promotes the build from dev to staging only when all CI quality gates (schema lint, prompt lint, version check) pass and the unit test suite is green. No manual approval is required.
Schema lint, prompt lint (safety baseline present, no secrets, input delimited), and version check (version bumped correctly for the type of change)
All unit tests pass with mocked LLM responses; no test failures or timeouts
Docker image or deployment package built successfully and pushed to the staging artefact registry
Gitleaks/Trufflehog scan finds no hardcoded credentials in any changed file

# .github/workflows/promote-dev-to-staging.yml
name: Promote Dev → Staging

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  ci-gates:
    uses: ./.github/workflows/ci-genai.yml   # gates 1-3 + unit tests

  build:
    needs: ci-gates
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Build and push image
        run: |
          docker build -t $IMAGE_REGISTRY/app:${{ github.sha }} .
          docker push $IMAGE_REGISTRY/app:${{ github.sha }}

  deploy-staging:
    needs: build
    environment: staging            # GitHub environment with no approval requirement
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Deploy to staging
        run: ./scripts/deploy.sh staging ${{ github.sha }}
      - name: Run eval suite
        run: python scripts/run_evals_ci.py --env staging
        env:
          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY_STAGING }}

Gate B promotes to the pre-production (canary) environment. It requires the eval suite to pass in staging and a smoke test to succeed, then automatically triggers deployment to pre-prod at reduced traffic (5% canary).
Pre-prod deploys at 5% traffic by default. The canary soak period is 24 hours before manual approval for full production promotion.

# .github/workflows/promote-staging-to-preprod.yml
name: Promote Staging → Pre-prod (Canary)

on:
  workflow_dispatch:          # triggered manually after eval suite passes
    inputs:
      sha:
        description: 'Image SHA to promote'
        required: true

jobs:
  check-eval-results:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      eval_passed: ${{ steps.check.outputs.passed }}
    steps:
      - id: check
        run: |
          RESULT=$(python scripts/check_eval_results.py --env staging --sha ${{ inputs.sha }})
          echo "passed=$(echo $RESULT | jq -r .passed)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

  deploy-canary:
    needs: check-eval-results
    if: needs.check-eval-results.outputs.eval_passed == 'true'
    environment: pre-prod         # requires tech lead + EM approval in GitHub
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Deploy canary at 5% traffic
        run: ./scripts/deploy-canary.sh preprod ${{ inputs.sha }} --traffic-weight 5
      - name: Start canary soak monitor
        run: python scripts/start_canary_monitor.py --duration-hours 24 --sha ${{ inputs.sha }}

During the 24-hour soak period, an automated canary analyser compares error rates, latency, and eval pass rates between the canary (5%) and stable (95%) traffic cohorts. If the canary degrades significantly, it is automatically rolled back.

# scripts/canary_analyser.py
# Runs continuously during the canary soak period.
# Compares canary vs stable cohort metrics every 15 minutes.
import time, requests, sys

DATADOG_API = "https://api.datadoghq.com/api/v1/query"
HEADERS     = {"DD-API-KEY": os.environ["DD_API_KEY"]}

def get_metric(query: str, minutes: int = 15) -> float:
    now  = int(time.time())
    resp = requests.get(DATADOG_API, headers=HEADERS, params={
        "from": now - minutes * 60,
        "to":   now,
        "query": query,
    })
    series = resp.json().get("series", [])
    if not series:
        return 0.0
    points = [p[1] for p in series[0]["pointlist"] if p[1] is not None]
    return sum(points) / len(points) if points else 0.0

def check_canary_health(sha: str) -> dict:
    canary_err  = get_metric(f"avg:llm.error_rate{{version:{sha},cohort:canary}}")
    stable_err  = get_metric(f"avg:llm.error_rate{{cohort:stable}}")
    canary_p95  = get_metric(f"avg:llm.latency.p95{{version:{sha},cohort:canary}}")
    stable_p95  = get_metric(f"avg:llm.latency.p95{{cohort:stable}}")

    issues = []
    if canary_err > stable_err * 2 and canary_err > 0.02:
        issues.append(f"Error rate degraded: canary={canary_err:.1%} vs stable={stable_err:.1%}")
    if canary_p95 > stable_p95 * 1.5:
        issues.append(f"Latency degraded: canary p95={canary_p95:.0f}ms vs stable={stable_p95:.0f}ms")

    return {"healthy": len(issues) == 0, "issues": issues}

# Run every 15 min for 24h
sha      = sys.argv[1]
deadline = time.time() + 24 * 3600
while time.time() < deadline:
    result = check_canary_health(sha)
    if not result["healthy"]:
        print(f"CANARY UNHEALTHY: {result['issues']}")
        # Trigger automatic rollback
        subprocess.run(["./scripts/rollback-canary.sh", sha], check=True)
        sys.exit(1)
    print(f"Canary healthy at {time.strftime('%H:%M')}")
    time.sleep(15 * 60)

print("24h soak complete — canary healthy")

Gate C is the most consequential gate: it promotes from a 5% canary to full production traffic. It requires manual approval from the engineering manager and, if the feature was security-flagged, the security lead.
24-hour canary soak completed with no automatic rollback triggered
Canary error rate ≤ stable error rate × 1.2 (within 20%)
Canary p95 latency ≤ stable p95 latency × 1.2
Operational Readiness Review (ORR) checklist fully signed off
On-call engineer briefed on the feature and the runbook
Rollback procedure tested in staging (kill-switch flag confirmed working)
Go/No-go meeting held with feature owner, tech lead, and EM

## Go/No-go: [Feature Name] Production Promotion

**Date/time:** [datetime]
**Attendees:** feature owner, tech lead, EM, [security lead if flagged]
**SHA being promoted:** [sha]

### Canary Results (last 24h)
| Metric          | Canary     | Stable     | Delta  | Status |
|-----------------|------------|------------|--------|--------|
| Error rate      | X.X%       | X.X%       | +X.X%  | ✓/✗   |
| p95 latency     | XXXms      | XXXms      | +XX%   | ✓/✗   |
| Cost/request    | $X.XXX     | $X.XXX     | +X%    | ✓/✗   |
| Eval pass rate  | XX%        | —          | —      | ✓/✗   |

### Open Items
- [ ] Any open P2+ incidents on the service?
- [ ] Rollback tested and timed (target ≤15 min)?
- [ ] On-call briefed?
- [ ] Alerts verified in staging?

### Decision
**GO / NO-GO:**
**Reason (if NO-GO):**
**Signed by:** EM: _______ | Tech lead: _______ | Security (if req.): _______

# .github/workflows/promote-preprod-to-prod.yml
name: Promote Pre-prod → Production

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      sha:
        description: 'SHA that completed canary soak'
        required: true
      go_nogo_doc:
        description: 'Link to signed Go/No-go document'
        required: true

jobs:
  verify-canary-complete:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Verify 24h soak completed
        run: python scripts/verify_canary_soak.py --sha ${{ inputs.sha }}

  promote-to-production:
    needs: verify-canary-complete
    environment: production         # Requires EM approval in GitHub Environments
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Gradual traffic increase (5% → 25% → 100%)
        run: |
          ./scripts/set-traffic-weight.sh production ${{ inputs.sha }} 25
          sleep 300    # 5 min observation
          python scripts/check_prod_health.py --threshold-minutes 5 || exit 1
          ./scripts/set-traffic-weight.sh production ${{ inputs.sha }} 100
      - name: Notify on-call
        run: python scripts/notify_oncall.py --message "Production promotion complete: ${{ inputs.sha }}"
Each gate's approval requirement is enforced through GitHub Environments, which gate workflow runs on named environments behind required reviewers.

When a prompt change is tagged with the security_review flag (because it introduces a new injection surface, processes PII, or triggers a compliance check), the production gate requires an additional approval from the security lead in addition to the EM.

# scripts/check_security_flag.py
# Reads changed prompt files and exits non-zero if any are security-flagged,
# triggering the security-review required-approver in the GitHub Environment.
import subprocess, yaml, pathlib, sys

changed = subprocess.check_output(
    ["git", "diff", "--name-only", "origin/main...HEAD", "--", "prompts/"]
).decode().splitlines()

flagged = []
for path_str in changed:
    path = pathlib.Path(path_str)
    if not path.exists():
        continue
    data = yaml.safe_load(path.read_text())
    if data.get("security_review", False):
        flagged.append(path_str)

if flagged:
    print("::warning::Security review required for:", ", ".join(flagged))
    print("::set-output name=requires_security_review::true")
else:
    print("::set-output name=requires_security_review::false")

sys.exit(0)  # not blocking — the GitHub Environment rule handles approval
A production rollback can be triggered by any on-call engineer without prior approval. It must complete within 15 minutes of the rollback decision.
P2 — investigate; rollback if not recovering
>5× hourly average with no traffic spike
P1 — likely runaway tokens; immediate rollback
Any sampled request fails safety filter in prod
Any observable degradation the engineer deems unacceptable

#!/bin/bash
# scripts/emergency-rollback.sh
# Usage: ./scripts/emergency-rollback.sh <environment> <previous-sha>
# Any on-call engineer can run this without approval.

ENV=$1
PREV_SHA=$2

if [ -z "$ENV" ] || [ -z "$PREV_SHA" ]; then
    echo "Usage: $0 <environment> <previous-sha>"
    exit 1
fi

echo "=== ROLLBACK: $ENV → $PREV_SHA ==="
echo "Started at: $(date -u)"

# Step 1: Disable feature flag (instant, < 1 min)
echo "Step 1: Disabling feature flag..."
./scripts/set-flag.sh llm_feature_flag off
sleep 30
./scripts/check-flag-health.sh || echo "WARNING: Flag health check failed"

# Step 2: Redeploy previous image (3–10 min)
echo "Step 2: Redeploying $PREV_SHA to $ENV..."
./scripts/deploy.sh "$ENV" "$PREV_SHA"

# Step 3: Verify rollback
echo "Step 3: Verifying rollback..."
python scripts/check_prod_health.py --sha "$PREV_SHA" --threshold-minutes 2
if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "ERROR: Health check failed after rollback. Escalate to #ml-platform immediately."
    exit 1
fi

echo "=== ROLLBACK COMPLETE ==="
echo "Completed at: $(date -u)"
echo "IMPORTANT: File an incident report and investigate root cause before re-promoting."
Every promotion event — automated or manual — must be logged to create an audit trail for compliance and post-incident analysis.

# Every promotion writes a structured log entry to the audit store.
import dataclasses, datetime, json

@dataclasses.dataclass
class PromotionEvent:
    event_type:        str       # "promotion" or "rollback"
    sha:               str
    from_env:          str
    to_env:            str
    timestamp_utc:     str
    triggered_by:      str       # GitHub Actions run URL or engineer username
    approvers:         list[str] # GitHub usernames who approved
    eval_pass_rate:    float | None
    canary_hours_soaked: float | None
    go_nogo_doc_url:   str | None
    rollback_reason:   str | None  # populated for rollback events only

def log_promotion(event: PromotionEvent, store: str = "s3://audit-logs/promotions/"):
    payload = dataclasses.asdict(event)
    filename = f"{event.timestamp_utc[:10]}-{event.sha[:8]}-{event.to_env}.json"
    # Write to audit store (S3, GCS, or your audit log service)
    print(json.dumps(payload, indent=2))
>90% of PRs promote within 30 min of merge
Investigate slow CI or eval failures
If >4h, check for blocked approvals or failed evals
If >2%, strengthen canary analysis or staging evals
Long meetings signal insufficient pre-gate validation
If higher, staging evals are not catching real regressions
Eval suite fails in staging but passed in CI
Eval cache hit in CI returned stale results
Invalidate eval cache on prompt content change; use SHA of prompt file as cache key
Staging traffic profile does not match production
Add production-representative traffic samples to staging load test
Go/No-go blocked for >2 days waiting for EM
EM approval not calendared; promotion treated as low priority
Schedule Go/No-go at sprint planning when release date is known; assign backup approver
Feature flag not tested; redeploy pipeline slow
Test rollback in staging as part of ORR; pre-bake images to eliminate build time
Security review adds >1 week to promotion
Security review not started until after ORR
Start security review as soon as PR is merged; run in parallel with staging eval
Canary soak silently passes despite degraded eval quality
Canary analyser only checks error rate and latency, not eval pass rate
Add sampled eval run to canary analyser; sample 1% of canary traffic through eval suite
GenAI applications require a four-environment promotion pipeline (Dev → Staging → Pre-prod/Canary → Production) with automated and manual gates at each boundary
Gate A (Dev → Staging) is fully automated: CI gates 1–3 must pass and the build must succeed
Gate B (Staging → Pre-prod) requires the eval suite to pass at ≥85% overall and 100% adversarial; it deploys a 5% canary that soaks for 24 hours
Gate C (Pre-prod → Production) requires a signed Go/No-go meeting, EM approval, and (if security-flagged) security lead approval; it promotes traffic gradually (5% → 25% → 100%)
Production rollback can be triggered by any on-call engineer without approval and must complete within 15 minutes; the kill-switch feature flag enables <1-minute partial rollback
Every promotion event is logged to an audit trail with approvers, eval results, and canary soak data for compliance and post-incident analysis
